q.en: According to the book, who was named the greatest athlete of the 20th century?
q.zh: 根据本书，谁被称为20世纪最伟大的运动员？

explain.en: The book says the athlete named the greatest of the great was Jim Thorpe, a Native American born in 1887.
explain.zh: 书中说被誉为最伟大运动员的是1887年出生的美国原住民吉姆·索普。

q.en: At the 1912 Olympics, what did Jim Thorpe win?
q.zh: 在1912年奥运会上，吉姆·索普赢得了什么？

options.en: Gold medals in both the pentathlon and decathlon
options.zh: 五项全能和十项全能两枚金牌

options.en: Nothing—he did not compete according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 什么都没有——他没参赛（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Thorpe won the pentathlon and set a world record in the decathlon, winning gold in both.
explain.zh: 书中说索普赢得五项全能，并在十项全能中创世界纪录，两项均获金牌。

q.en: After excelling in basketball, track, and field, in which sport did Babe Didrikson become most famous?
q.zh: 在篮球、田径表现出色后，贝布·迪德里克森在哪项运动中最出名？

explain.en: The book says Babe's real fame came in golf; she won eighty-two tournaments including seventeen in a row.
explain.zh: 书中说贝布真正的名声来自高尔夫，她赢得82场赛事，包括1946—47年连续的17场。

q.en: In 1947, what barrier did Jackie Robinson break in Major League Baseball?
q.zh: 1947年，杰基·罗宾逊在职业棒球大联盟打破了什么壁垒？

options.en: The color barrier as the first African American player
options.zh: 作为首位非裔美国人球员打破种族壁垒

options.en: The pitching speed record according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 投球速度纪录（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Robinson became the first African American to play Major League Baseball, breaking the color barrier.
explain.zh: 书中说罗宾逊成为首位出战职业棒球大联盟的非裔美国人，打破了种族壁垒。

q.en: At the 1936 Berlin Olympics, how many gold medals did Jesse Owens win?
q.zh: 在1936年柏林奥运会上，杰西·欧文斯赢得了几枚金牌？

options.en: Ten according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 十枚（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Jesse earned four gold medals in Berlin, a first in Olympic history.
explain.zh: 书中说杰西在柏林赢得四枚金牌，为奥运史上首次。

q.en: Why was Jesse Owens' participation in the 1936 Olympics controversial?
q.zh: 为什么杰西·欧文斯参加1936年奥运会充满争议？

options.en: Nazi leaders believed Aryans were superior and called black people 'primitive'
options.zh: 纳粹领导人认为雅利安人更优越，并称黑人为'原始'

options.en: He was too old to compete according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他年龄太大无法参赛（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book explains the Nazis believed Aryans were better than everyone else, making Owens' success controversial.
explain.zh: 书中解释纳粹认为雅利安人优于其他人，因此欧文斯的成功充满争议。

q.en: Which team did Jim Thorpe join as a professional baseball player in 1913?
q.zh: 1913年，吉姆·索普作为职业棒球运动员加入了哪支球队？

explain.en: The book says in 1913 Thorpe became a professional baseball player with the New York Giants.
explain.zh: 书中说1913年索普成为纽约巨人队的职业棒球运动员。

q.en: What did Babe Didrikson do that was unusual for women of her time?
q.zh: 贝布·迪德里克森做了什么在当时女性中很不寻常的事？

options.en: She refused to play sports according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她拒绝参加运动（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She lifted weights and was a serious, rough competitor
options.zh: 她举重，并且是认真而强硬的竞争者

explain.en: The book notes Babe lifted weights, which few women did then, and was a serious competitor when women were expected to be ladylike.
explain.zh: 书中指出贝布举重（当时女性很少做），并且在女性被期望温婉的年代是个认真的竞争者。

q.en: What group did Jackie Robinson's courage help change in professional sports?
q.zh: 杰基·罗宾逊的勇气帮助改变了职业体育中的什么？

options.en: It opened the door for black and Latino players to participate equally
options.zh: 为黑人和拉丁裔球员平等参与打开了大门

options.en: It created only women's teams according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 只创建了女子队（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says Robinson showed skill mattered, and soon other teams began hiring black and Latino players.
explain.zh: 书中说罗宾逊证明实力才是关键，不久其他球队也开始雇用黑人和拉丁裔球员。

q.en: What is the main reason the book remembers these American sports legends?
q.zh: 本书纪念这些美国体育传奇的主要原因是什么？

options.en: Their skill, courage, and breaking of barriers changed sports
options.zh: 他们的技艺、勇气和打破壁垒改变了体育

options.en: They were all born in the same city according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们都出生在同一座城市（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They all played only one sport
options.zh: 他们都只参加一项运动

explain.en: The book highlights how each athlete's determination and breaking of barriers made them legends worth remembering.
explain.zh: 本书强调每位运动员的决心与打破壁垒，使他们成为值得铭记的传奇。

prompt.en: If you could interview one of these athletes, who would it be and what would you ask them about their greatest challenge?
prompt.zh: 如果可以采访其中一位运动员，你会选谁？你会问他/她关于最大挑战的什么问题？

en: Pick one athlete from the book and recall a challenge they faced.
Think of a thoughtful question that shows you understood their story.
zh: 从书中选一位运动员，回想他/她面临的挑战。
提出一个能体现你理解其故事的有深度的问题。
